steps.title: Explain the odd result
steps.content: Patel's team searched the Scopus and Web of Science databases for the term "Twitter" while reviewing studies of the social media website. Most returned papers did focus on the platform, but a few discussed unrelated subjects such as sounds made by tractor engines. The blank must explain why papers about engine sounds would appear in a keyword search for "Twitter."

steps.title: Remove unrelated explanations
steps.content:
• Choice A says researchers discussed tractor engines on the social media platform, but that would not explain why academic database records about engine sounds matched the search term.
  • Choice B describes the databases generally and does not explain the specific off-topic results.
  • Choice D concerns academics announcing publications on Twitter, not papers about engine sounds.

steps.title: Use the word meaning
steps.content: Choice C supplies the missing link: the word "twitter" can refer to something other than the social media website, including noises made by machines. If papers used the word in that sound-related sense, a database search for "Twitter" could return them. The answer is C.
